sections.name: Spawn Of Jeharlu
From Jeharlu Spores

link.alt: Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight pg. 89
link.name: Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight pg. 89

sources.name: Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight

sections.text: Treerazer uses Jeharlu Spores as his opening gambit against Kyonin in an attempt to spread fungal blights in a sudden and explosive manner. Those same spores, left to germinate in the Outer Rifts, instead develop into spawns of Jeharlu.

link.alt: Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight pg. 89
link.name: Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight pg. 89

sources.name: Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight

sections.text: Spawns of Jeharlu typically drift in the skies above the realm of Jeharlu, content to enjoy blasphemous and horrific dreams and fancies while they watch the fungal world below for intruders. It isn't that far off the mark to equate a spawn of Jeharlu to a white blood cell, except in this case the body they defend is Jeharlu. Encountered beyond this realm, a spawn's role reverses—now they play the part of infective agents, seeking to spread corruption through soil, vegetation, and fleshy life wherever they go.

link.alt: Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight pg. 89
link.name: Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight pg. 89

sources.name: Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight

traits.name: Rare
traits.text: This rarity indicates that a rules element is very difficult to find in the game world. A rare feat, spell, item or the like is available to players only if the GM decides to include it in the game, typically through discovery during play. Creatures with this trait are rare. They typically can't be summoned. The DC of Recall Knowledge checks related to these creatures is increased by 5.

traits.name: Fiend
traits.text: Creatures that hail from or have a strong connection to the unholy planes are called fiends. Fiends can survive the basic environmental effects of planes in the Outer Sphere.

traits.name: Fungus
traits.text: Fungal creatures have the fungus trait. They are distinct from normal fungi.

traits.name: Unholy
traits.text: Effects with the unholy trait are tied to powerful magical forces of cruelty and sin. They often have stronger effects on holy creatures. Creatures with this trait are strongly devoted to unholy causes, and often have weakness to holy. If a creature with weakness to unholy uses an unholy item or effect, it takes damage from its weakness.

automatic_abilities.name: Hallucinatory Spores
automatic_abilities.text: The spawn is surrounded by spores that manipulate the thoughts of those exposed. When a creature ends its turn in the aura and isn't holding its breath, it must attempt a DC 42 Will save. If it fails, it becomes confused until the end of its next turn (or for 1 minute on a critical failure).

traits.name: Aura
traits.text: An aura is an emanation that continually ebbs out from you, affecting creatures within a certain radius. Aura can also refer to the magical signature of an item.

traits.name: Divine
traits.text:
This magic comes from the divine tradition, drawing power from deities or similar sources. Anything with this trait is magical.  
  
A creature with this trait is primarily constituted of or has a strong connection to divine magic.

traits.name: Emotion
traits.text: This effect alters a creature’s emotions. Effects with this trait always have the mental trait as well. Creatures with special training or that have mechanical or artificial intelligence are immune to emotion effects.

traits.name: Mental
traits.text: A mental effect can alter the target’s mind. It has no effect on an object or a mindless creature.

automatic_abilities.effect: A flurry of tendrils whips out, allowing the spawn of Jeharlu to attempt an Athletics check to Grapple the triggering creature. The spawn can Pull the creature if it remains grappled on its next turn.
automatic_abilities.name: Grasping Strands
automatic_abilities.trigger: A creature within 60 feet of the spawn of Jeharlu uses a move action or leaves a square during a move action it's using

interaction_abilities.name: Scent Purity
interaction_abilities.text: A spawn of Jeharlu can detect holy creatures with scent as a precise sense to 120 feet.

traits.name: Forceful
traits.text: This weapon becomes more dangerous as you build momentum. When you attack with it more than once on your turn, the second attack gains a circumstance bonus to damage equal to the number of weapon damage dice, and each later attack gains a circumstance bonus to damage equal to double the number of damage dice.

traits.name: Magical
traits.text: Something with the magical trait is imbued with magical energies not tied to a specific tradition of magic. Some items or effects are closely tied to a particular tradition of magic. In these cases, the item has the arcane, divine, occult, or primal trait instead of the magical trait. Any of these traits indicate that the item is magical.

traits.name: Reach
traits.text: This weapon can be used to attack enemies up to 10 feet away instead of only adjacent enemies. For creatures with reach, the weapon increases their reach by 5 feet.

traits.name: Unholy
traits.text: Effects with the unholy trait are tied to powerful magical forces of cruelty and sin. They often have stronger effects on holy creatures. Creatures with this trait are strongly devoted to unholy causes, and often have weakness to holy. If a creature with weakness to unholy uses an unholy item or effect, it takes damage from its weakness.

traits.name: Versatile
traits.text: A versatile weapon can be used to deal a different type of damage than its listed type. This trait indicates the alternate damage type. For instance, a piercing weapon with versatile S can deal piercing or slashing damage. You choose the damage type each time you attack.

universal_monster_ability.effect: The monster attempts to Reposition the creature, moving it closer to the monster. This attempt neither applies nor counts toward the monster's multiple attack penalty. If Pull lists a distance, change the distance the creature is pulled on a success to that distance.
universal_monster_ability.name: Pull
universal_monster_ability.requirement: The monster's last action was a success with a Strike that lists Pull in its damage entry

ability.critical_failure: As failure, but double damage and the target becomes doomed 1.
ability.failure: The target takes full damage and is drained 2. If it was already drained, its drained condition value increases by 1 to a maximum of drained 4.
ability.name: Soul
ability.success: The target takes half damage and becomes drained 1.
ability.text: The spawn extends feeding stalks to drain the spirit and life from an adjacent restrained or helpless creature. The target takes 10d6 spirit damage and must attempt a DC 45 Will save. If the damage from Drain Soul reduces a creature to 0 Hit Points, that creature dies instantly.

traits.name: Death
traits.text: An effect with the death trait kills you immediately if it reduces you to 0 HP. Some death effects can bring you closer to death or slay you outright without reducing you to 0 HP.

traits.name: Divine
traits.text:
This magic comes from the divine tradition, drawing power from deities or similar sources. Anything with this trait is magical.  
  
A creature with this trait is primarily constituted of or has a strong connection to divine magic.
